Package org.apache.juneau.bean.hal

Class HalLink

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.bean.hal.HalLink
@Marshalled public class HalLink extends Object
Represents a HAL Link Object as defined by draft-kelly-json-hal-08 § 5.

A Link Object describes a hyperlink from the containing resource to another resource. The href property is the only required field; all other fields are optional.

See Also:
  • HAL Link Object
  • juneau-bean-hal

    • HalLink

      public HalLink()
      Default constructor.

    • HalLink

      public HalLink(String href)
      Convenience constructor with the required href field.
      Parameters:
      href - The link URI or URI template.

  • Method Details

    • getHref

      public String getHref()
      Bean property getter: href.

      The link's target URI or URI template (per RFC 6570). This is the only required HAL Link field.

      Returns:
      The value of the href property, or null if it is not set.

    • setHref

      public HalLink setHref(String value)
      Bean property setter: href.
      Parameters:
      value - The new value for this property.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • getTemplated

      public Boolean getTemplated()
      Bean property getter: templated.

      Indicates whether href is a URI template per RFC 6570.

      Returns:
      The value of the templated property, or null if it is not set.

    • setTemplated

      Bean property setter: templated.

      value - The new value for this property.
      Returns:
      This object.

      public String getType()
      Bean property getter: type.

      A hint about the media type expected when dereferencing the target resource.

      Returns:
      The value of the type property, or null if it is not set.

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Bean property getter: name.

      A secondary key for selecting a link from a set sharing the same relation.

      Returns:
      The value of the name property, or null if it is not set.

      The value of the profile property, or null if it is not set.
